rationale_en: Financed one of Iran's flagship hydroelectric dams at a time when hydro capacity was small, so a single project's completion could visibly shift the hydro share of total electricity.
One dam's financing is a real but small contribution to national gross capital formation, which aggregates investment across the whole economy.
Corporate farms were a notable structural experiment but covered only a limited share of farmland, so the expected effect on aggregate agricultural production is marginal.
A narrow land-consolidation scheme with limited reach has no material expected effect on the broad rural-development category.
Foundational tourism-sector statute, but its effect on actual tourism receipts is real and partial, filtered through geopolitics and sanctions rather than a direct visible break.

Placed the entire private banking system under state administration, a major structural instrument for the monetary and banking-access domains even if less direct for central-bank government debt specifically.

A central pillar of revolutionary restructuring belongs in GDP's historical narrative, but administrative bank takeover is a monetary-sector event with only marginal direct effect on aggregate output.
One of many routine supplementary budget amendments of the era, a small force within the government-finance domain.
Foundational accounting statute defining how the state records and classifies every budget figure, so its adoption would be expected to produce a visible definitional break in reported budget totals.
Narrow bylaw implementing a single budget clause, procedural and administrative with no expected effect on budget totals.
A tax-administration compliance directive that broadens the base incrementally, one of many such instruments rather than a major revenue driver.
Narrow bylaw implementing a single budget clause, procedural with no expected effect on budget totals.
Narrow bylaw implementing a single budget note-clause, procedural with no expected effect on budget totals.
Sets the formal methods for state-enterprise divestiture including stock-exchange sale, a real but partial channel among several driving listed-company and market-cap figures.
A procedural methods bylaw rather than the substantive divestiture decision, so its marginal effect on net investment in nonfinancial assets is limited.
Governed a large, well-known subsidized-credit job-creation scheme explicitly targeting self-employment and unemployment, a real but partial force among several shaping the labor market.
The scheme channeled significant subsidized credit, but it remains one component within the much larger aggregate of private-sector credit.

Direct regulatory framework for a non-bank credit segment that later grew large and destabilizing, a real material channel into private-credit and deposit series though less direct for commercial bank branch counts.
Narrow bylaw implementing specific budget notes, procedural with no expected effect on budget totals.
Directs bank resources toward specific infrastructure loans, a real but small channel against aggregate private credit to GDP.
Directed infrastructure financing is one contributor among many to national gross fixed capital formation.
A small-scale incentive scheme for industrial research permit holders, administrative in nature with no material expected effect on sectoral value added.
Targeted housing subsidy for a narrow beneficiary population, negligible against the national housing-stock series.
Narrow bylaw implementing a single budget clause, procedural with no expected effect on budget totals.
Narrow multi-sector implementing bylaw for several budget note-clauses, administrative detail with no expected effect on budget totals.
Justice Shares was a large nationwide asset-redistribution program, a real but partial and often delayed channel into household wealth given realized dividends frequently fell short.
Redistributing existing state-enterprise shares nominally expands free float but does not create new capital, a marginal effect on aggregate market capitalization.
A national subsidized-credit and fast-track licensing scheme explicitly designed to boost new business registration, a real though partial driver alongside broader economic conditions.
Framed as a job-creation program, but its scale is modest relative to Iran's total labor force and aggregate unemployment.
Narrow bylaw implementing specific budget note-clauses, procedural with no expected effect on budget totals.
Narrow bylaw implementing specific budget note-clauses, procedural with no expected effect on budget totals.
Sets a payment mechanism for asset transfers rather than the underlying divestiture decision, a marginal channel into net investment in nonfinancial assets.
An indirect and speculative channel from an installment-payment mechanism to broader household wealth accumulation, negligible expected effect.

Buy-back contracts are Iran's principal legal vehicle for foreign investment under constitutional ownership restrictions, so extending them to non-oil sectors is a real but partial channel given historically modest non-oil FDI volumes.
A speculative, indirect channel from non-oil buy-back investment to aggregate industrial value added, no material expected effect.
Narrow bylaw implementing a single budget note-clause, procedural with no expected effect on budget totals.
Narrow bylaw implementing a single budget note-clause, procedural with no expected effect on budget totals.
Narrow bylaw implementing a single budget note, procedural with no expected effect on budget totals.
A jurisdictional ruling on which official administers Justice Shares does not itself move listed-company market capitalization.
An administrative-authority ruling with no expected effect on household wealth distribution.
A single utility service-fee ruling is one small basket item with no expected visible effect on aggregate CPI.
The fee-affordability link to physical access to clean cooking fuel is speculative and negligible.
A municipal fee ruling on bank-branch openings is a minor cost item with no material expected effect on branch network expansion.
A narrow billing-currency dispute over one company's aviation fuel sales does not set FX policy and has no expected effect on the aggregate exchange rate.
Concerns the distributing company's billing practice, not import volumes, so no material expected effect on oil product imports.
A technical clarification of taxable-income computation for bank branches, administrative with no expected effect on aggregate income tax revenue.
A small municipal duty tariff schedule, negligible against aggregate other-taxes revenue.
A minor local business-premises fee has a speculative and negligible link to new business registration.
A municipal service tariff on industrial estates is a minor cost item with no expected effect on aggregate industrial value added.
A speculative pass-through from a local service fee to the wholesale price index, negligible expected effect.
A single-municipality duty on fuel stations is far too narrow to move national taxes on goods and services.
GSTP accession is a real preferential-tariff commitment, but the scheme is historically known for very limited practical utilization, so expected effect on customs revenue is marginal.
GSTP's actual trade volume impact among developing-country members was minimal, negligible expected effect on merchandise trade.
As one of the earliest codified annual budget acts, its own totals are literally data points within the Annual Budget Law Totals series it targets.
Inaugurated the recurring national annual-budget institution, a real but partial contributor to the broad government-finance category rather than a single defining event.
A routine annual budget law is, per the standard budget-versus-GDP relationship, a small force against the much larger national economy.
Formalized cross-border trade with neighboring countries, a real but partial channel into recorded merchandise trade and customs duty collection given many other trade routes exist.
Appears to be a routine annual appropriation for IMF dues and contributions rather than a quota-defining agreement, a marginal channel into use of IMF credit.
The direct, foundational regulatory instrument for fisheries and aquaculture licensing and catch rules, a major sector-wide instrument with material expected effect on production.

Established the main national farm-credit institution, a real but partial contributor to agricultural value added alongside land reform, irrigation and other policies, and only a marginal slice of total private credit.
Directly channels financing, land and tariff mechanisms specifically toward urban and suburban rail, the enabling instrument behind this exact investment and network-expansion series.
An ownership-nationalization of one warehousing company is a real but indirect channel into customs duty collection and clearance efficiency, not a change to duty policy itself.
Directly regulates logging, grazing and land-use rights, the core legal framework shaping forest area and depletion, a major instrument with material expected effect.
Foundational public accounting law setting the classification and reporting rules for the entire government-finance domain, whose adoption would be expected to produce a visible reporting break.
Conditions how government expenditure is recorded, a definitional channel into GDP's expenditure side, but its effect cannot be separated from underlying yearly spending decisions.
An early historical budget act from an era with almost no comparable chart data, procedural and negligible against modern GDP.
A reallocation amendment within the Fourth Development Plan rather than the plan itself, an administrative adjustment with only marginal expected effect on GDP or capital formation.

The state took over the entire private banking system, a structural break expected across banking and monetary aggregates, tempered slightly here because GDP itself is only one of several bundled, more indirect targets.
The direct legal act converting every private bank into a state institution, structurally reorganizing exactly what the monetary and banking-access categories measure.
A financing mechanism compensating landlords within the land-reform program, narrower and more indirect than the reform itself, with only marginal expected effect on GDP or agricultural land.

The core White Revolution land-reform statute is central to Iran's mid-century economic history, but redistributing ownership does not mechanically change total farmland area or output, consistent with the low expected causation of land reform on GDP-adjacent series.
The explicit statute restructuring rural land tenure, directly reorganizing the land-holding patterns that the agriculture and rural-development category is built to track.
A named instrument authorizing higher crude export volume, a real policy lever though actual export levels are also bound by field capacity, OPEC quotas and geopolitics.
Authorizes Caspian Sea exploration, but Iran's Caspian sector has historically yielded minimal actual production, so expected effect on national oil output is marginal.
A routine supplementary adjustment within the ongoing consortium-era oil and gas sale framework, not a structural change to production or export volumes.
Duplicate entry of the same routine supplementary consortium-era agreement, same marginal expected effect.
A single foreign joint-venture agreement with one company is small in scope against national oil production totals.
A security and criminal-penalty statute protecting oil infrastructure is background context, not a driver of production levels.
Sarcheshmeh is Iran's dominant copper operation, and this land transfer directly enabled its site development, a major instrument with material expected effect on national copper output.
Sets a ceiling on one debt instrument among several channels of government and central-bank liability creation, a marginal constraint on the aggregate.
Authorizes one bond-financing instrument that historically represented a modest share of total government debt and money supply, a real but partial channel.
Near-duplicate of the 2007 participation-bond issuance act, same modest partial channel into debt and money supply.

Corporatizing provincial power distributors is an institutional restructuring step, but by itself it does not mechanically drive private investment, loss reduction or access without further follow-through.
A direct government credit line targeted specifically at livestock development, a real but partial driver alongside weather, disease and market conditions.
A bilateral preferential-tariff agreement with a single trading partner is a small slice of Iran's aggregate MFN tariff rate and total merchandise trade.
A single-clause technical amendment to the customs tariff schedule, negligible against aggregate import duty revenue.
A narrow single-article amendment to a customs-duty refund scheme for domestic factories, administrative with no material expected effect.
A single-article addition to the general export/import framework, procedural with no expected effect on aggregate trade volumes.
Cold-storage infrastructure funding reduces post-harvest losses in distribution, not the aggregate food production index itself.
A targeted housing loan facility for a narrow beneficiary population has no material expected effect on national housing tenure patterns.

A direct infrastructure-financing channel for sewage treatment and urban water networks, a real but partial and gradual contributor to national drinking-water access alongside broader urban development.
One concrete mechanism within Iran's broader multi-channel divestiture program, not the dominant driver of listed-company counts or market capitalization.
A narrow amendment to a share-transfer mechanism for a specific beneficiary group has no material expected effect on GDP.
A single-article bylaw amendment adjusting wartime trade-licensing operations, procedural with no expected effect on merchandise trade.
A routine bylaw amendment within the general export/import regulatory framework, procedural with no expected effect.
A technical single-article bylaw amendment to trade-licensing procedure, negligible expected effect.
Opened private-sector mineral extraction during a period of severely constrained state mining investment, a real but partial contributor since state entities still dominated the sector.
A supervisory compliance-monitoring instrument that, per its own description, does not set production incentives.
Fixes the reference prices used to compute the implicit energy subsidy each year, a substantive channel directly shaping the subsidies line, though only one clause within the wider budget totals.
One subsidized-credit job-creation scheme among several under the Third Development Plan, a marginal contributor to aggregate unemployment and private credit figures.
